projectDescription: An interactive, visually immersive single-page web app celebrating Pi Day (March 14) with educational content, a digit-memory game, data visualizations, and an AI-style chatbot — all wrapped in a dark sci-fi aesthetic.
keyFeatures: Interactive circle circumference visualizer with real-time radius slider
Three-mode Pi digit memory game (Normal, Time Attack, Sudden Death)
Animated history timeline from Babylonians to modern supercomputers
Data gallery showing the first 1,000 Pi digits as a colored matrix or circular star map
PiBot floating chatbot with hardcoded Q&A about Pi
Easter egg: typing '3.14159' anywhere triggers a confetti celebration
Sci-fi dark theme with glassmorphism panels, neon glow effects, and Framer Motion animations
Fully responsive layout with mobile navigation drawer
Deployed to Vercel via vercel.json configuration
publicApiSurface: Hero section — animated π symbol with scrolling digit ribbon
WhatIsPi section — interactive circle with radius slider, circumference formula
History section — scroll-driven animated timeline with 6 historical milestones
MemoryGame section — keyboard-driven digit input, three game modes (normal, time, endless)
Visualizer section — 1,000-digit grid/circle toggle with per-digit color legend
RealWorld section — six real-world Pi application cards
PiBot component — floating chat widget with preset Q&A
EasterEgg component — global keydown listener triggering confetti on '3.14159'
NeonButton UI component — primary/secondary/accent variants with glow
Navbar — fixed header with smooth-scroll anchor links
PI_DIGITS constant — first 1,000 digits of Pi
DIGIT_COLORS constant — neon color map for digits 0–9
